law: The Linux ISO image you download must support the CPU architecture of your Mac.
When you run the app for the first time, it displays a file picker so you can choose the Linux installation ISO image to use for installing your Linux VM.
When the installation finishes, the Linux VM is ready to use.
Running multiple VMs requires an app to manage the execution and artifacts of each individual VM.

The example below shows the steps for configuring and to enable this capability: To use the copy-and-paste capability in Linux, the user needs to install the package, which is available through most Linux package managers.
The sample configures the boot loader that the virtual machine requires to run the guest operating system, and it configures a console device to handle standard input and output.
The kernel and RAM disk image must support the CPU architecture of your Mac.

The sample configures the virtual machine to use two CPUs and 2 GB of RAM.
It also sets the object’s property with additional information about how to use the RAM disk and console information.
You can use this tool to download the restore image of the most current macOS release from the network, or with your own restore image.
You use to launch and control the macOS VM that loads and runs macOS from the VM bundle.
This app includes entitlements to enable it to use Virtualization and access the VM’s’ audio input, such as the microphone.
You can use either version, they’re functionally identical.
The Virtualization framework checks the configuration to make sure it supports saving and restoring.
